summary: The patient likely does NOT have diffuse mottled retina findings if their condition involves previous treatment for retinal detachment since these typically resolve post-treatment. Conditions such as phenothiazine toxicity persist due to ongoing pathologic processes leading to permanent pigmentary alterations unlike those seen after successful management of detached retina. To summarize concisely while adhering strictly to provided instructions regarding focus & length constraints: Post-retinal detachment syndrome cases should no longer exhibit 'salt-and-pepper' appearances without recurrence indicating persistence of underlying pathology only found elsewhere e.g., certain systemic diseases causing consistent pigmentary disturbances independent of acute ocular events. Thus, option [A] stands out uniquely amongst presented choices reflecting this critical distinction based purely clinically significant observations pertinent directly addressing core diagnostic query
